title: GitHub Actions
content:
### GitHub Actions Overview

GitHub Actions automates software workflows directly in your repository. Build, test, and deploy right from GitHub.

### Workflow YAML

Workflows are defined in `.github/workflows/*.yml`:

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test
```

### Triggers

- `push` — Runs when code is pushed
- `pull_request` — Runs on PR events
- `schedule` — Cron-based scheduling
- `workflow_dispatch` — Manual trigger

### Jobs and Steps

- **Jobs** — Independent units that can run in parallel or sequentially (`needs:`)
- **Steps** — Individual commands or actions within a job

### Runners

- **GitHub-hosted** — Ubuntu, Windows, macOS (updated regularly)
- **Self-hosted** — Your own infrastructure

### Marketplace Actions

Community-contributed actions available on the [GitHub Marketplace](https://github.com/marketplace/actions) — checkout, setup languages, deploy, and more.
codeExample:
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm test

quiz.question: Where are GitHub Actions workflow files stored?
Which event triggers a workflow on a scheduled basis?
